element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Basic traversal and weak enemy dispatch
Primary ranged weapon for early levels
High-damage area denial and heavy enemy engagement
Special weapon for crowd control and utility
Utility weapon that reduces enemy size and threat
Movement speed boost and Mighty Foot enhancement
Vertical movement and access to elevated areas
Primary recurring enemy type throughout game
Climactic episode conclusions requiring learned weapon/movement mastery
Level exploration and hidden cache discovery

typical_relief_triggers: Environmental interaction moments (light switches, water fountains)
typical_tension_triggers: Limited health resources in combat areas
rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Non-linear level design allows player-driven tension pacing; combat intensity escalates through episode progression with boss climaxes; exploration-focused rhythm with periodic combat surges

spatial_grammar.optional_content_signal: Air ducts, back doors, sewers explicitly designed as non-linear alternate routes; jet pack and vertical traversal enable optional exploration of elevated areas
spatial_grammar.secret_convention: Hidden caches and health resources placed in destructible environments and off-path locations; interactive props signal explorable space

difficulty_curve.early_game: Introduction to pistol, shotgun, and basic Pig Cop enemies in destructible Hollywood environments; environmental interaction mastery encourages exploration
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Chaingun and RPG introduction; increased enemy variety and density; hazard traversal (hot/toxic terrain, underwater sections); HoloDuke and protective equipment as tactical layers
difficulty_curve.late_game: Freezethrower, shrink ray, laser mines, and Devastator mastery; boss encounters requiring learned weapon and movement patterns; jet pack enabling complex vertical navigation; multiple simultaneous advanced enemy types
notable_spikes: Episode boss encounters (culmination of learned skills)
Introduction of RPG weapon and multiple heavy enemy types simultaneously
Atomic Edition Episode 4 introduction (expansion pack difficulty increase)

notable_level_patterns.description: Levels designed with air ducts, back doors, and sewers enabling players to navigate around enemy concentrations or discover hidden health/ammo caches; rewards exploration and resource optimization
Interactive props (light switches, breakable walls, water fountains) create secondary discovery loops separate from combat flow; environmental feedback encourages systematic level examination
Final level of each episode features boss encounter requiring mastery of all previously introduced weapons and movement mechanics; clear arena demarcation from exploration-focused earlier levels
Sections requiring protective boots (hot/toxic terrain), aqua-lung (extended underwater), or jet pack (vertical gaps) as mandatory progression tools; forces equipment switching and tactical planning
Lighting-dependent sections where darkness obscures enemy positions and navigation; night vision goggles transform threat assessment; optional challenge without mandatory light switch use
